briefing.opening: Good morning/afternoon. I will now begin the climate briefing for March 22, 2026.

sections.title: 🔴 Global Climate Crisis Status and Immediate Impacts
sections.content: In East Africa, droughts and floods have occurred simultaneously for five consecutive years, leading to a tragic situation of food crisis and climate refugees affecting 20 million people [6, 7]. These climate changes are causing significant health crises in African health systems [28]. In Colorado, USA, ten signs of climate change have already been observed, and experts warn that extreme weather events such as torrential rains in Hawaii, rare snow in Alabama, and severe heatwaves on the West Coast are striking the US, revealing the 'fingerprints' of the climate crisis [25, 27, 36]. Furthermore, concerns about the worsening climate crisis are escalating with the raised possibility of a recurrence of the El Niño phenomenon [35], and domestically, the disappearance of large birds has been identified through indigenous knowledge as a warning of a biodiversity crisis [3].

sections.title: 🌱 Accelerating Carbon Neutrality and Renewable Energy Transition
sections.content: Domestic and international companies and local governments are actively responding to climate change. Hyundai Steel is continuously advancing its climate change response system [1], and one company received the highest rating from CDP for two consecutive years, recognizing its climate change response efforts [2]. Incheon Dong-gu achieved first place in environmental and climate administrative performance, demonstrating outstanding results in practicing carbon neutrality [5], and Daejeon Yuseong-gu conducted river cleanup and carbon neutrality campaigns in commemoration of World Water Day [10, 17]. As high oil prices persist, the importance of new and renewable energy is further highlighted, and renewable energy is rapidly emerging in conjunction with increasing AI power demand [12, 19, 22, 23, 24]. Furthermore, international cooperation efforts are continuing, with Apple and China sharing common goals such as innovation, eco-friendliness, and carbon neutrality [18].

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Exploring Climate Response Strategies for Domestic Industries and Local Communities
sections.content: Domestic industries are seeking survival strategies amidst the dual challenges of high oil prices and carbon neutrality [12, 13]. While the urgent need for introducing carbon emission reduction equipment in industrial complexes and supporting the demonstration of eco-friendly ships is emphasized [15], Jeju Province continues its efforts towards global carbon neutrality [9]. In local communities, the Chungnam Provincial Council is promoting the cultivation and enhanced support for climate change-resilient crops [4], and Gimhae City is striving to nurture future talent through cooperation in training 'Glocal Carbon Neutrality Leaders' [11]. The Bucheon Volunteer Center is encouraging citizen participation by hosting a 'Carbon Neutrality Volunteer Mini-Expo' [14], demonstrating that practical climate change response efforts are underway in various regions.

sections.title: 🌍 Expanding Global Research and Discussion on Climate Change
sections.content: Scientific efforts are also continuing, with groundbreaking research results being announced, discovering natural ways to combat climate change [26]. Discussions are also underway on how to find and protect 'natural climate change safe zones' where wildlife can thrive [29], and regarding the common belief that tree planting is a simple solution to stop global warming, science points out that it's not that simple, emphasizing the importance of a complex approach [33]. Meanwhile, discussions on various policy approaches are also active, with opinions being raised that litigation is not the right way to address climate change [34]. The importance of future generations' participation and education is also being emphasized, with 8th-grade students from Unity Charter School receiving recognition for climate change leadership from the New Jersey Department of Education [32].

briefing.closing: Today's briefing has highlighted the current state of the global climate crisis, which is intensifying worldwide, while also demonstrating the active deployment of various efforts and strategies, both domestically and internationally, to overcome it. Through continued attention and cooperation, we must effectively respond to climate change. This concludes today's climate briefing.

summary.phenomenon: In March 2023, the United States is experiencing a series of extreme weather events, including torrential rains in Hawaii, rare snowfall in Alabama, fluctuating temperatures in the Northeast, and a severe heatwave on the West Coast.
